qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via Host header injection in AppController::actionResourceJs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets an unauthenticated SSRF in AppController::actionResourceJs using Host-derived @web, a raw str_starts_with baseUrl check, and a Guzzle fetch. The advisory targets authenticated RCE in ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud where fieldLayouts is passed to FieldLayout::createFromConfig() without cleanseConfig(), enabling Yii behavior/event injection.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is an SSRF in AppController::actionResourceJs, not the advisory's ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud fieldLayouts behavior-injection RCE.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2fph-6v5w-89hh describes authenticated RCE through ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud passing fieldLayouts to FieldLayout::createFromConfig() without Component::cleanseConfig().

craftcms_appcontroller_ssrf/vulnerability_report.json describes an unauthenticated SSRF through AppController::actionResourceJs, Request::getHostInfo(), AssetManager baseUrl prefix validation, and Craft::createGuzzleClient()->get($url).
The finding report does not mention ElementIndexesController::actionFilterHud, fieldLayouts, FieldLayout::createFromConfig(), cleanseConfig(), Yii as/on behavior keys, or the advisory RCE chain.
